title: triticum caninum subsp. gracilius
intro: Triticum caninum subsp. gracilius, also known as wild wheat or slender dog wheat, is a perennial grass native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating. The health benefits of Triticum caninum subsp. gracilius include its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and promote overall well-being.
